title: THE PATTERN CRITICS KEEP REWARDING
subtitle: The Late Peak Theory
body: Three acclaimed films, three delayed climaxes, one pattern emerges. "Air," "American Fiction," and "Conclave" all peak between 85-95% through their narratives. The data reveals critical darlings consistently withhold maximum tension until final moments. Late-peaking structure correlates strongly with 93%+ Rotten Tomatoes scores across diverse genres and storytelling approaches.
